mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.50% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower cost of capital for corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of April 2016 indicates deep easing stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market strength and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days through increased aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,462 thousand as of April 2016
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to confirm demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610k units and RSAFS at 373,103M as of March 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 2% MoM, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO < 3-month MA are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.1% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through lower corporate earnings and demand contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of April 2016 remains below 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.0% are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.2% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.50%) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) creates financial repression conditions that boost real asset returns including equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.37 as of March 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both conditions hold, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.0% annualized signals robust economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through multiple expansion and earnings upgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,260.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2015
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when GDPC1 growth > 2.0% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.8% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 5.0%) signals tight supply conditions and potential wage-price spiral, which the Fed must combat through rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate monetary tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.19 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of March-April 2016
regime_hint: tight labor and supply conditions with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both INDPRO growth > 0.5% and UNRATE < 5.0%, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
